name: Seeed reTerminal
description: Raspberry Pi CM4-based all-in-one HMI with integrated 5" capacitive touchscreen, sensors, and modular rear expansion bay

model.comment: Two SKUs differ only in CM4 RAM (4 GB vs 8 GB); both pair the CM4 with 32 GB eMMC. RESEARCH.md candidate line bundles 'reTerminal / reComputer R1100' but those are distinct products — the reComputer R1100 is a headless DIN-rail IoT gateway with no integrated screen. The 'integrated screen + carrier' description matches the reTerminal, so this entry covers the reTerminal proper.

release-date.comment: Announced 15 Apr 2021; pre-orders opened end of April 2021.

status.comment: 8 GB CM4108032 SKU listed In Stock at $259 on Seeed store; 4 GB CM4104032 SKU listed Out of Stock at the same vendor (checked 2026-04-26). Product line is still actively sold and the newer reTerminal DM 10" continues the family.

architecture-family.comment: Broadcom BCM2711, ARMv8-A; runs both 32-bit and 64-bit Raspberry Pi OS images.

open-source-hardware.comment: OSHW-RPi-Series repo only publishes PDF/ZIP schematics and an .STP 3D model — no native PCB design files (KiCad/Eagle/BRD). Per RESEARCH.md Assessment Guidelines this counts as partial openness, not OSHW.

country-of-origin.comment: Seeed Studio is HQ'd in Shenzhen.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Stock CM4 clock; community overclock to 2.0–2.2 GHz works but is not vendor-supported.

process-node.comment: BCM2711 is fabricated on TSMC 28 nm — inherited from the Raspberry Pi 4 / CM4 die.

opengl-es-version.comment: BCM2711 / VideoCore VI is OpenGL ES 3.1 conformant via Mesa V3D.

vulkan-support.comment: Vulkan 1.2 conformant via the Mesa V3DV driver (open-source, in-tree).

hw-video-decode.comment: BCM2711 hw-decodes H.265 up to 4Kp60 and H.264 up to 1080p60. No VP9 or AV1 hw-decode.

hw-video-encode.comment: H.264 1080p30 hw-encode only; no H.265/VP9/AV1 encode block.

ram-min-gb.comment: Smallest currently-sold reTerminal SKU is the CM4104032 (4 GB CM4).

ram-max-gb.comment: Largest currently-sold SKU is the CM4108032 (8 GB CM4). Seeed has not shipped 1 GB or 2 GB CM4 variants of the reTerminal.

ram-type.comment: CM4 uses LPDDR4-3200 with on-die ECC.

emmc.comment: 32 GB eMMC on the CM4 module; soldered, not removable.

microsd-slot.comment: All shipped reTerminal SKUs use the eMMC-equipped CM4, which physically disables the microSD path; the reTerminal chassis exposes no microSD slot.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: No onboard M.2 slot. The rear vertical expansion connector exposes the CM4's PCIe lane; an optional E10-1 backpack carries SATA, but no NVMe M.2 carrier is sold.

sata.comment: No native SATA on the reTerminal itself; SATA is only available via the E10-1 expansion backpack.

wifi.comment: Dual-band 2.4/5 GHz 802.11b/g/n/ac (Wi-Fi 5) via the CM4's Cypress/Infineon CYW43455 radio.

bluetooth.comment: Bluetooth 5.0 BLE via the CM4's onboard radio.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: All exposed USB-A ports run at USB 2.0 speed.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: Single USB-C is power-only (5 V / 3 A); not a data port.

poe-support.comment: No PoE on the base device. The optional E10-1 expansion backpack adds PoE, but it is a separate board mating on the rear connector — not a HAT, and not on the SBC itself.

hdmi-outputs.comment: Single Micro-HDMI port (in addition to the integrated 5" 1280×720 internal display).

max-display-resolution.comment: Refers to the Micro-HDMI output (4Kp60 from the CM4). Internal LCD is 5" 1280×720.

mipi-dsi.comment: CM4 carries DSI internally to drive the bonded 5" panel; no user-accessible DSI connector is exposed on the reTerminal chassis.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm analog audio output on the chassis. Audio out is via HDMI or via the E10-1 expansion.

gpio-pins.comment: 40-pin Raspberry Pi-compatible header on the rear; 26 of those pins are GPIO per the standard RPi pinout (the rest are power and ground).

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Pin-compatible with the standard 40-pin RPi GPIO.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Header is electrically compatible but mounted on the back of an enclosed device — there is no Pi-standard mechanical room (mounting holes, board clearance) to mount a HAT directly. Per RESEARCH.md note 6 this is mechanical-only / electrical compatibility, not true HAT compliance.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 1× PCIe 2.0 via proprietary rear expansion connector
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: CM4's single PCIe Gen 2 lane is routed to the rear high-speed expansion connector (used by E10-1 etc.); not a standard M.2 or mPCIe slot.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, and UART all available on the 40-pin header (up to 5× of each via the CM4's flexible muxing).

idle-power-w.comment: Measured at stock 1.5 GHz with screen on (Dickson Chow review). Excludes external peripherals.

load-power-w.comment: Measured under CPU stress at stock 1.5 GHz; rises to ~7.1 W when overclocked to 2.1 GHz.

cooling-required.comment: Vendor enclosure is passive — no fan, no heatsink shipped. CM4 is BCM2711 in a closed ABS case; sustained heavy load will throttle but will not require active cooling for typical HMI workloads.

rtc-battery-header.comment: An NXP PCF8563T RTC chip is onboard, but the chassis exposes no coin-cell holder or external RTC battery header.

form-factor-class.comment: Bonded enclosure with integrated 5" touchscreen; not a bare PCB and does not match any of the listed Pi/ITX form-factor classes.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Chassis-level mounting (panel-mount style) rather than Pi-standard PCB mounting holes.

weight-g.comment: Bare reTerminal device weight including enclosure, screen, and CM4.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: CM4 (BCM2711) has had full mainline support since Linux 5.16. The reTerminal-specific extras (touchscreen, accelerometer, light sensor, RTC) are standard ICs (Goodix, LIS3DH, LTR-303, PCF8563) with mainline drivers; Seeed publishes overlays for them. Audio path through the HDMI / I2S is upstream.

official-os-image.comment: Seeed publishes preconfigured 32-bit and 64-bit Raspberry Pi OS images for the reTerminal, plus instructions for stock RPi OS / Ubuntu.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor publishes a board-specific Raspberry Pi OS image; Ubuntu (64-bit) is officially documented to work via Seeed's flashing guide. Armbian for the RPi 4 family is still WIP and not a board-specific reTerminal image, so omitted.

bootloader.comment: Inherits the Raspberry Pi VideoCore boot ROM + closed-source bootcode/EEPROM firmware chain used by all CM4 / BCM2711 boards. No U-Boot or UEFI on the standard image.

long-term-support.comment: No formal vendor LTS commitment, but Seeed has actively maintained images and overlays since the 2021 launch (~5 years), and the CM4 itself has a Raspberry Pi commitment to 2034. Capping at 3-5y because Seeed's own software-support window is not contractually stated.

android-support.comment: No vendor-maintained Android image for the reTerminal. Community LineageOS / Android-on-CM4 ports exist for the Pi 4 family but are not specific to this board.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Seeed list price for the 4 GB CM4104032 SKU as of 2026-04-26 (currently OOS at Seeed). Original 2021 launch price was $195 for the 4 GB model; pricing has risen with CM4 cost.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Seeed list price for the 8 GB CM4108032 SKU as of 2026-04-26 ($259, marked down from $359). Both base and top SKUs are currently priced equally on the Seeed store, which is unusual — likely a clearance discount on the 8 GB unit.

available-regions.comment: Seeed ships from China, US, and Germany warehouses; also resold on Amazon US, Botland (EU), Kiwi Electronics (EU), RobotShop (NA/EU).

typical-lead-time.comment: 8 GB SKU In Stock at Seeed as of 2026-04-26; 4 GB SKU is currently Out of Stock at Seeed but available via resellers (Amazon, Botland).

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive Seeed wiki (getting-started, hardware-interfaces, FAQ, flashing guides), published schematics (PDF), and a 3D STP model. Falls short of 5 because no native PCB design files and no full datasheet for the chassis-integrated peripherals beyond the wiki write-up.

community-size.comment: Seeed has a large general community, but the reTerminal is a niche HMI product within it. Discussion is concentrated on the Seeed forum, GitHub issues, and a handful of dedicated reviewers — well under the orange-pi / odroid scale.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run Seeed Studio forum has an active reTerminal section.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: First-party E10-1 expansion backpack (DC/PoE/UPS, LTE/4G/5G/LoRaWAN, RS485/232, CAN, SATA) plus optional cameras and a few community modules. No vibrant third-party ecosystem of cases or HATs because the device is a sealed enclosure — most RPi accessories cannot mount on it.
